raw_output_payload.reasoning: Need grep -R "foo" /system | tee? Actually output to console and count lines. Use grep -R "foo" /system | tee /dev/stderr? Wait console is stdout. We can pipe to tee? But we want to display matches and then number of matched lines. Use grep -R "foo" /system | tee /dev/stderr? No. Simpler: grep -R "foo" /system | tee >(wc -l) but that would output count separately. We can use grep -R "foo" /system | tee /dev/tty | wc -l? But we need to print matches then number. Use grep -R "foo" /system | tee /dev/tty | wc -l? That would print matches then count. But tee writes to stdout and /dev/tty. Actually tee writes to stdout and file. We can use: grep -R "foo" /system | tee /dev/tty | wc -l. But that will output matches then count. However tee will output matches to stdout, then wc -l reads from tee output? Wait pipeline: grep | tee /dev/tty | wc -l. tee writes to stdout and /dev/tty. wc reads from tee's stdout, counting lines. So final output will be count only, not matches? Actually tee writes to stdout, which goes to wc, so matches not displayed. But we also want matches displayed. Use: grep -R "foo" /system | tee /dev/tty | wc -l. This will display matches to terminal via /dev/tty, and wc output count. That works. Provide command.
